title: Manager frontend core refactor
constraints: All touched source and docs files must remain UTF-8 encoded.
Preserve current route paths, admin gating, and backend API contracts.
Do not mix broad copy rewrites with structural refactors.
Keep npm run build passing after each major task.
Add regression coverage for the new shared primitives and route behavior.

tasks.title: Route-level code splitting
tasks.goal: Reduce initial manager bundle size by lazy-loading page routes behind a shared fallback.
public_interfaces: App routes keep the same public paths and AdminRoute behavior.
steps: Write a failing route rendering test that expects the shared fallback while a lazy page module is unresolved.
Run the targeted test to confirm the current route tree does not provide the fallback behavior.
Convert page imports in App.tsx to React.lazy and wrap route elements with Suspense using a shared spinner-based fallback.
Run the targeted test and the manager build to confirm behavior and bundle output stay valid.
verification: cd manager/frontend && npm run test -- --runInBand route-lazy
acceptance_criteria: Current paths still resolve to the same pages.
Admin-only routes still redirect non-admin users.
The app shows a shared fallback while a lazy route is loading.

tasks.title: Shared navigation and section primitives
tasks.goal: Replace duplicated tab-strip and section shell markup with reusable UI primitives.
public_interfaces: SectionCard props: title, subtitle, actions, children, contentClassName
steps: Write failing component tests for ResponsiveTabs active state switching and SectionCard header rendering.
Run the targeted tests to confirm the new components do not exist yet.
Implement the shared components and replace repeated tab-strip markup in Config, Admin, and Reports.
Run the new tests plus a manager build to confirm the shared components behave consistently on existing pages.
verification: cd manager/frontend && npm run test -- --runInBand responsive-tabs section-card
acceptance_criteria: Config, Admin, and Reports use the same responsive tab implementation.
Repeated card header shells can be rendered through SectionCard.
No page loses its existing tab labels or page header behavior.

tasks.title: Normalize frontend API access
tasks.goal: Move raw page-level fetch calls behind dedicated API modules that share auth and error handling.
steps: Write failing API-focused tests that assert page components call the dedicated modules instead of raw fetch.
Run the targeted tests to confirm the current implementation still reaches fetch directly.
Implement the dedicated API modules and migrate Login, Templates, and Admin stock-cache flows to use them.
Run the targeted tests and a manager build to confirm behavior is preserved with normalized error handling.
verification: cd manager/frontend && npm run test -- --runInBand api-normalization
acceptance_criteria: Touched pages no longer contain duplicated authenticated JSON fetch logic.
Existing endpoint paths and payload shapes remain unchanged.
File export remains a navigation/download flow rather than JSON fetch.

tasks.title: Decompose Reports page
tasks.goal: Split the oversized reports page into focused modules without changing report behavior.
public_interfaces: Reports.tsx remains the page coordinator for tab selection and date filter state.
Section modules receive only the data inputs they need, preserving tab ids and visibility rules.
steps: Write failing regression tests for Reports tab switching, admin-only NL logs visibility, and date-filter visibility on period-sensitive tabs.
Run the targeted tests to confirm they fail against the current monolithic page behavior contract.
Extract report sections and shared helpers into focused modules while keeping Reports.tsx as the coordinator.
Run the regression tests and a manager build to confirm the refactor preserved page behavior.
verification: cd manager/frontend && npm run test -- --runInBand reports-page
acceptance_criteria: All current report tabs still exist with the same ids and labels.
The period filter still appears only for period-sensitive tabs.
Admin-only NL logs remain hidden from non-admin users.

tasks.title: Add frontend test baseline
tasks.goal: Introduce repeatable regression coverage for routing, tabs, navigation, and page-local state.
public_interfaces: npm run test becomes the standard frontend verification command.
steps: Write failing tests for App admin gating, BottomNav pinned entries, ResponsiveTabs state switching, and Config tab switching.
Run the targeted tests to confirm the new test command and setup are not wired yet.
Add Vitest and React Testing Library setup, then make the new tests pass against the current component contracts.
Run the new frontend test suite and manager build to confirm the baseline is stable.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend tests can run locally without live backend access.
At least one admin-only route and one localStorage-backed UI behavior are covered.
The new test command is documented through package.json scripts.

tasks.title: UTF-8 hygiene on touched files
tasks.goal: Keep touched manager frontend files valid UTF-8 and repair confirmed broken Korean literals encountered during the refactor.
public_interfaces: No behavior changes beyond corrected visible strings and comments.
steps: Audit the touched files that showed garbled Korean during inspection and write failing UI tests where corrected copy is important.
Run the targeted tests to confirm the current visible strings are wrong or unstable.
Repair only confirmed broken literals/comments in touched files and save them as UTF-8.
Run the frontend build and tests again to confirm the cleanup did not introduce regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Corrected Korean strings render properly in the affected UI surfaces.
No unrelated copy rewrite is mixed into the structural refactor.
